title: PROTECT Jewish Student and Faculty Act
short_title: PROTECT Jewish Student and Faculty Act
plain_summary: This bill makes colleges and universities add rules against antisemitism to their student and employee handbooks if they want to keep getting federal money for student aid. Schools must include a clear definition that explains antisemitism as hatred toward Jewish people and Jewish places like synagogues. The schools also have to say that antisemitic behavior is not allowed on campus and could lead to students being kicked out or employees being fired. If colleges don't follow these rules, they could lose their federal student aid funding.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Promote Restoring Order To End Campus Targeting of Jewish Students and Faculty Act or the PROTECT Jewish Student and Faculty Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Prohibition of Antisemitic Conduct
sections.text: This section changes an existing education law called the Higher Education Act of nineteen sixty five. It adds a new requirement for colleges and universities. If these schools want to keep receiving federal student aid money, they must follow this new rule. The rule says that colleges and universities must include certain information in all their documents about how students and employees should behave on campus. First, they must include a definition of antisemitism. This definition says that antisemitism is a certain way of thinking about Jewish people that may show up as hatred toward Jews. It explains that antisemitic words and actions can be aimed at Jewish people or non Jewish people, including damage to their property. It can also be aimed at Jewish community buildings and religious places like synagogues. Second, these schools must include a clear statement that antisemitic behavior is not allowed on campus. The statement must explain the consequences. If a student engages in antisemitic conduct, the student may be kicked out of the school permanently. If an employee engages in antisemitic conduct, the employee may be fired from their job at the school.
